methods.description: The calcination method is used to remove organic matter and structural water in diatomaceous earth at a suitable temperature, optimizing the material's properties for purification applications.

steps.description: Determine the appropriate calcination temperature and time. The optimum calcination temperature is in the range of 500℃ to 800℃, and the time is controlled in the range of 0.5 h to 2 h. This article uses 600℃ calcination for two hours.
steps.searchable_summary: set calcination temperature to 600℃ and time to 2 hours

steps.description: Perform calcination at 600℃ for two hours.
steps.searchable_summary: execute calcination process at 600℃ for 2 hours

steps.description: Capture scanning electron micrographs of diatomaceous earth before and after calcination.
steps.searchable_summary: capture SEM images of diatomaceous earth pre- and post-calcination

steps.description: Analyze the specific surface area of diatomite with different shapes after purification.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze specific surface area of calcined diatomite

methods.description: Testing the formaldehyde purification performance of diatom purification materials, focusing on adsorption rates and long-term effectiveness.

steps.description: Measure the formaldehyde purification rate of diatom purification materials over time.
steps.searchable_summary: measure formaldehyde adsorption rate of diatom materials

steps.description: Analyze the formaldehyde purification lasting rates of diatom purification materials.
Visualize the formaldehyde purification rate curve and test results.
steps.searchable_summary: create visualizations of purification rate and test results
